title: Bloodhound Scout Architecture Decision
summary: Chose standalone bloodhound.py module (not capability inside Miner) for clean separation: Bloodhound operates on the corpus pre-Miner-bootstrap, independent of mining mechanics. Supports future headless scout-only mode without loading Miner machinery.
decision: Bloodhound is a standalone class/module; Miner receives its output as a scalar top_well string at construction time. No bidirectional coupling.

title: Bloodhound Empirical Proof-of-Concept
summary: K482 Root anchored to 'shipped' (first file LB-CAT.M-0001 was about shipped features). K486 Bloodhound scanned 189 files in 0.05s and identified 'founder' as highest-density Well (21 files, density 1.0). Root now anchors to 'founder', not the first-file artifact.

title: multi_well_scores Forward-Only Design
summary: K486 adds multi_well_scores to bedrock tablets. Decision: forward-only (only tablets written AFTER a sibling's spawn get scored against it). Retroactive scoring rejected because it would require re-hashing the IP-ledger chain, invalidating the hash-chain provenance guarantee from K482.
decision: Forward-only multi_well_scores. Retroactive scoring architecturally prohibited.

title: Daughter Cross-Reference: NOT Re-Mining
summary: Daughters do not re-mine prior bedrock. claim_cross_references() scans existing bedrock JSONL files, scores each tablet against daughter.primary_topic using multi_well_scores (fast path) or live keyword scoring (fallback), and writes a cross-reference index JSONL. Tablets remain parent-owned; daughter has a claim-link only.
provenance_model: parent_serial unchanged; daughter records (tablet_id, parent_miner_serial, score, source_file) in cross_references/<daughter_serial>.jsonl

title: K486 vs K482 Baseline Comparison
summary: K486 run on 189 files (corpus grew 7 files since K482). K486: 12 Miners, 11 mitosis events, 2177 tablets. K482: 12 Miners, 11 mitosis events, 2275 tablets (delta: -98 tablets; corpus had fewer files active per Miner due to pre-anchor changing early file distribution). Mitosis pattern qualitatively similar but root differentiation achieved.

title: Module Background Task IPC Pattern
summary: K486 extends the Helm PWA module API with background task support: main process receives module:run IPC, spawns Python subprocess with piped stdio, broadcasts stdout/stderr line-by-line via module:output:<taskId> channel, tracks status in moduleTasks Map. Renderer uses useModuleTask() hook to subscribe to output and control lifecycle.

title: Module Lifecycle: Stop on Disable
summary: V0 decision: if user disables a module mid-run, the stop button terminates the subprocess (SIGTERM). No pause/resume in V0. State is preserved in the JSON files on disk; a future re-run picks up where the corpus is (though it does a fresh mining run). Pause+persist+resume is K487+ scope.

title: Bloodhound Clustering Algorithm Choice
summary: Bloodhound uses naive dominant-keyword clustering (file assigned to cluster of its top keyword). Considered TF-IDF global IDF weighting and k-means clustering; rejected both as over-engineering for a pre-pass tool that just needs to identify the densest topic area. Dominant-keyword clustering is O(n) and deterministic.

title: Sculptor End-to-End with K486 Bedrock
summary: Sculptor ran on K486 bedrock (2177 tablets). All 6/6 Sculptor verification checks passed. public-wide: 76.4% pass rate (1663/2177). technical-deep: 55.5% (1208/2177). private-founder: 89.0% (1938/2177). IP-as-filter keystone #28 confirmed operational. Filter decision log: 20181 entries.

title: Cross-Reference Threshold Calibration
summary: Default threshold 0.40 chosen for daughter cross-reference claims. At 0.40: 3024 total cross-references across 11 daughters. 'founder' daughters claimed ~881 tablets each (makes sense: founder is the corpus's dominant topic, so most tablets have some founder signal). 'attribution' daughter claimed only 3 (narrow-topic well). Threshold exposed as CLI arg for tuning.

title: Helm Module Registry Extended: Three New Modules
summary: registry.ts now registers: bloodhound (tools, v1.0.0), miner (tools, v1.0.0), sculptor (cathedral, v1.0.0). All three disabled by default. Appear in Modules panel under their categories with toggle cards. Enabling renders the corresponding React component in the main area.

title: useModuleTask Hook Design
summary: Shared React hook for all three Helm module components. Manages: state machine (idle/running/done/error), output line buffer, exit code, PID tracking. Listens to module:output:<taskId> IPC channel. Detects [task-exit] sentinel line to transition states. Clean teardown via useEffect cleanup.

title: K486 Run Performance
summary: Full K486 pipeline (Bloodhound + 189 files mining + 11 daughters cross-referencing): 2.64s total wall time. Bloodhound scout: 0.05s. Mining 2177 tablets: ~1.5s. Cross-reference (3024 claims, 11 daughters): ~1.1s. This is fast enough that the Helm module run button can be expected to return within seconds for typical corpus sizes (<500 files).

title: Module Output Color-Coding in Helm UI
summary: MinerModule.tsx applies semantic color-coding to output stream: mitosis events in amber (#f59e0b), Bloodhound lines in purple (#a78bfa), cross-reference lines in green (#34d399), stderr/error lines in red (#f87171), normal stdout in muted (#94a3b8). BloodhoundModule and SculptorModule use similar semantic coloring for their domain-specific log patterns.

title: K486 MODULE_API.md Extension Scope
summary: MODULE_API.md specified background-process modules as K486+ scope. K486 delivers: backgroundEntrypoint pattern (via ipcMain.handle module:run), ipcChannel namespace (module:output:<taskId>), and process supervision (spawn + exit handler + SIGTERM). Full permissions model deferred to K487+.

title: Window.helm Type Declaration Update
summary: App.tsx window.helm interface extended with four new module-task signatures: runModuleTask, stopModuleTask, getModuleTaskStatus, onModuleOutput. All four bridge to corresponding ipcRenderer.invoke / ipcRenderer.on calls in preload. Renderer code is fully type-safe via this interface.

title: Miner snapshot() Cross-Reference Count
summary: snapshot() in miner.py now includes cross_ref_count: reads the daughter's cross_references/<serial>.jsonl at snapshot time and counts non-empty lines. Adds cross-reference stats to population snapshot and run summary miner_table without requiring cross-reference data to be stored in memory during mining.

title: Sculptor Dual-Output (Artifacts + Eblets) Confirmed
summary: Sculptor K483+K485 code already supports dual-substrate (bedrock tablets AND synapse Eblets). Running on K486 bedrock produced 3 cathedral artifacts (SC-001 public-wide, SC-002 technical-deep, SC-003 private-founder) plus the filter decision log (20181 entries). Eblet mode is gated on Synapse substrate input — requires a synapse feed to be running.

title: Active Wells Registry Design
summary: Module-level _ACTIVE_WELLS dict in miner.py stores serial→primary_topic for all spawned Miners. register_active_well() called by run harness on each Miner/daughter spawn. Tablets written AFTER registration score against all registered wells. Design is single-process-scoped — sufficient for the CLI run harness; multi-process Helm runs would need IPC-based registry.
design: _ACTIVE_WELLS module global, register_active_well() API, forward-only forward

title: Bloodhound Single-File Cluster Inclusion Heuristic
summary: Bloodhound's default min_cluster_files=2 was tuned: if fewer than 3 multi-file clusters exist, single-file clusters are included to avoid an empty candidates list. This handles small corpora where every topic appears once. For Bishop corpus (189 files), 10+ multi-file clusters exist so the heuristic does not trigger.

title: Helm Build Verification K486
summary: npm run build (electron-vite) completed with exit 0 after all K486 changes. 38 renderer modules transformed, 606 kB renderer bundle, 10.27 kB main bundle, 1.34 kB preload bundle. No TypeScript errors. Three new .tsx modules and one .ts hook included in bundle.
